assumptions: The dividend receivable is an intragroup monetary item denominated in a currency different from the relevant entity's functional currency.
The foreign exchange differences on that intragroup monetary item are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
The group can meet IFRS 9 hedge documentation and effectiveness requirements at inception of the hedging relationship.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a genuine hedge accounting route because IFRS 9 allows a recognised asset or liability to be designated in a fair value hedge, and intragroup monetary items are specifically eligible in consolidation when their foreign currency risk is not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a distinct hedge designation route because IFRS 9 also permits a recognised asset or liability to be hedged for cash flow variability attributable to a particular risk, provided that risk affects profit or loss at the consolidated level.
